story.iWant: a project sidebar that displays my personal and shared projects with a refresh button
story.soThat: I can easily navigate between projects and manually fetch the latest updates
story.tasks: - Implement Sidebar Layout and Type-Based Grouping (My Projects, Shared Projects sections) - Implement Project Navigation (click handler, route to detail view, active state) - Implement Action Buttons ("New Project" and "Refresh" buttons) - Implement Cache-Driven Updates (subscribe to cache invalidation events) - Implement Pending Invitations Badge (show count, navigate to invitations view) - Performance Optimization (virtualization for long lists, render <100ms for 100 projects) - Testing (grouped list, refresh button, navigation, accessibility)

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Sidebar displays project list grouped: "My Projects" (type=personal) and "Shared Projects" (type=shared)
Each project shows: icon, name, color indicator (from Moku Web palette)
Clicking project navigates to project detail view
"Refresh" button visible and invalidates cache when clicked
Project list updates when cache invalidated (manual refresh)
Pending invitations badge shown with count

doc.section: ProjectSidebarUI: Display project list with refresh button, handle navigation - Inputs: User's projects (owned + shared) - Outputs: Project selection events, refresh events - Position: Primary sidebar section (Architecture §8.1)
Project Entity with type field: { type: 'personal' | 'shared' } Moku Web color palette: 12 predefined colors (#3B82F6, #10B981, #F59E0B, etc.)

1. User clicks "Refresh" button 2. ProjectService.refresh() invalidates caches: ProjectCache.invalidatePattern('projects:*') 3. Fresh data fetched from API: GET /api/projects 4. Cache updated with new 5-minute TTL 5. UI updates with latest projects (reactive)
- Sidebar render: <100ms for 100 projects - Project list retrieval: <300ms (cache hit), <1s (cache miss) - Refresh operation: <2s (P95)

code.file: Existing sidebar component pattern Will add project section with type-based grouping
Project list item component (may already exist) Shows icon, name, color indicator
Project store for reactive state Subscribe to updates from ProjectService
ProjectService.loadProjects() and refresh methods IPC calls to electron backend

dependencies.dependency: IPC for project list and refresh

constraints.constraint: Manual refresh only (no automatic polling in MVP)
Moku Web color palette (12 predefined colors)

interface.name: ProjectSidebarUI
interface.method: Invalidates cache and fetches fresh data

interface.name: IPC
interface.method: Manual refresh - invalidates cache and fetches fresh
Fetches pending invitations for badge count

tests.standards: - Unit tests: Component rendering, grouped list logic, button handlers - E2E tests: Navigation, refresh button, pending invitations badge - Performance tests: Render time benchmarks with 10/50/100 projects - Accessibility tests: Keyboard navigation (Tab, Enter), ARIA labels, screen reader

ideas.idea: Test grouped list: filter projects by type, render "My Projects" and "Shared Projects" sections
Test project item displays icon, name, and Moku Web color indicator
Test "Refresh" button calls ProjectService.refresh() and invalidates cache
Test project list reactively updates when cache invalidated
Benchmark sidebar render with 100 projects, verify <100ms
Test pending invitations badge shows correct count
Test performance with large lists: 500 projects grouped by type
